summary: Follow-up noted while reconciling the unified current audit plan across schema compatibility, queue diagnostics, freshness/release gates, and the fast-smoke developer loop.

observations.problem: Supply-chain report generation can still encounter local virtualenv or cache path quirks in Windows-backed workspaces even though release manifest and freshness scanning now have explicit cache and dev-hidden exclusion policies.
observations.why_it_matters: The supply-chain reports are part of the release evidence path, so a local environment traversal failure can block otherwise valid schema and queue contract work from closing cleanly.
observations.automation_candidate: Factor a shared repository traversal exclusion helper and reuse it from CycloneDX/OpenVEX source discovery, release manifest construction, and artifact freshness scans.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-04-26 by hardening public export traversal and adding a supply-chain pipeline regression that patches .venv/lib64 as inaccessible while asserting CycloneDX and OpenVEX generation still completes.
